name: School District 3
description_short: Widefield School District 3 is a public K-12 school district in Colorado Springs serving 5,600+ students with flexible learning options and community programs.
description_long:
Widefield School District 3 (WSD3) is a public school district located in Colorado Springs, Colorado, serving the Widefield area. The district operates as part of the public education system and focuses on providing K-12 education with a stated mission of helping "Every Child, Every Classroom, Every Day" learn, grow, and achieve. The district maintains an active web presence at https://www.wsd3.org/ and regularly engages with families through events and communications.

WSD3 offers comprehensive public K-12 education including preschool through high school programming. The district hosts transition nights to support students moving between grade levels (PreK to Kindergarten, Elementary to Middle School, Middle to High School) and provides supplementary programs including free summer meals and community events. The district also operates multiple schools including Sproul Junior High School and maintains partnerships with local facilities like Security Public Library for hosting events.

The district distinguishes itself through community-focused initiatives, including free sensory inclusion nights for individuals of all abilities, book donation programs (5,600+ books distributed), back-to-school support events, and parent engagement programs like the DAAC meetings. WSD3 emphasizes inclusive education and provides support services such as childcare at family events and programs focused on student empowerment.

As a public school district, WSD3 is fundamentally an educational institution, not a financial services provider. The miscategorization as a "payday-alternative" appears to be a data entry error. While the district may offer various services to families and students, it operates as a government education agency funded through public tax dollars, not as a consumer finance company offering loans, credit products, or financial alternatives.
services: K-12 public education (PreK through High School)
pros: Free summer meal programs for students

Free transition night events for families changing grade levels
Back-to-School Bash providing student support and school supplies

Active book distribution program (5,600+ books given to students)
Inclusive programming designed for students of all abilities
cons: This is a public school district, not a financial services provider—miscategorized on CreditDoc

No loan, credit, or payday alternative products available through this organization
best_for: Families with children in the Widefield, Colorado Springs area seeking K-12 public education
Students needing free meal programs and community support services
Families looking for inclusive education options for children with sensory or developmental needs
diagnosis: Widefield School District 3 is a public K-12 school district and should not be listed on a consumer finance platform. This represents a fundamental miscategorization—the organization provides public education services, not financial products or payday alternatives. Removal from CreditDoc's database or recategorization to an educational institution directory is appropriate.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
